Estonia, swipe right
on this Product Manager

I’m a computer engineer & entrepreneur turned product manager ready to change my life and yours. Join me on my journey to rescue 10 injured animals, bring your vision to life, and help make Estonia the best country in the world.

Read more

Backstory

Five years ago, I was bitten by a bug – not the kind that gives you superpowers (sadly), but the kind that pushes you forward into the entrepreneurial unknown. Three successful companies later, I’m ready for a new challenge.

The goal? A position as product manager with a startup in Estonia. I’ve heard you’re looking for talent, and I’d love to fill the gap.

Why hire me?

#1

Extensive experience in key areas of product management - 6 years and counting...

  • Founder of 3 profitable startups: Ressy Multia Metaworks
  • Manager of teams of up to 25 professionals in development, design, and marketing
  • Provide full life cycle management of mobile and web applications
  • Strong UI/UX skills
  • Forge strong business partnerships and employee relationships

#2

Strong communication and technology skills

Education Bachelor of Engineering, Information Technology Education evaluated by World Education Services

Download report

International English Language Test System Evaluates reading, listening, writing, speaking skills

IELTS Score - 8/9

Download report

#3

Intelligent use of data to drive product improvements

If I’m not collecting and using data to inform my decisions, I’ve probably been kidnapped and replaced by a body double. Knowing what data to study and how to implement changes accordingly is one of the most crucial skills I’ve developed.

I’ve worked to compliment that with ‘soft skills’ - building relationships with customers, providers, and employees - while keeping a close eye on real information about the product we’re working on.

Want to know how I improved merchant acquisition costs and onboard time by 53% and 42%, respectively, at my previous company?

Case study

How will I help you?

Vision & Execution

At its core, my mission as a product manager is to help businesses turn ideas into profitable, scalable products. So, I’ll pull out my binoculars – and my magnifying glass – to make sure we’re on track for the “big picture” while we hit every project milestone along the way.

Team Management

Inform stakeholders? Check. Get engineering to talk to marketing? Check. ...Check.

3rd cup of morning coffee?

Your team deserves someone who’s ready to bat for them every morning (no matter how many cups of coffee it takes). I pride myself on being a relatable, approachable manager who can facilitate communication and help each team overcome the obstacles they face.

Customer Advocacy

You know a great way to avoid *headdesk* moments after product launch? Putting customers first. Making sure that their use cases and our features mesh. Keeping their interests in mind every meeting.

I make it my mission to see that every project I work on is in the best interests of customers and company alike. Who doesn’t want a win-win?

Accountability

In all seriousness, I take full ownership of each project I manage. An open, honest environment leads to happy employees who invest their energy into a better product and a more profitable company. I wouldn’t have it any other way.

Flexibility – and fun

I’m looking for a new adventure in Estonia and want to make a positive impact in the live of each person I meet, one day at a time. If that means moving across the world, learning new technologies, and forging new friendships, then count me in.

Make me buy a one-way ticket to Estonia

Traveling across the world. Taking the helm on a groundbreaking project. A firm handshake when we hit every goal you set.

That’s what I want. What about you?

If I get hired by an Estonian startup, I promise to rescue 10 injured animals in collaboration with ResQ.

Download Resume Schedule a Call

RESQ Charitable Trust is a not-for-profit organisation that works towards the rescue and rehabilitation of injured and sick animals, conducting awareness and education programs which focus on impacting society with respect to reducing human-animal conflict and conservation of the environment.
RESQ is a registered Animal Charity under the Bombay Public Trusts Act, 1950 and registered under Section 12 (A) and 80 (G) of the Income Tax Act, 1961.

Pranks
Help enterprenuers from disadvantaged communities

Case study: Reducing merchant acquisition and onboard costs for Ressy

Ressy is a location-based restaurant application that aggregates all local restaurant deals in real time by partnering with restaurants (merchants).

It then provides a platform for banks, credit card companies, and mobile wallets to provide significant discounts (up to 50% off) to their users.

In other words, it helps our clients offer sweet deals to their clients. But getting restaurants to partner with us took a lot of time and money. It’s hard enough to corral a busy owner, let alone convince them that

Initial merchant acquisition cost: ~$32

Initial time to on-board merchant: 5-7 days

My first step was to learn everything

I could about the current merchant acquisition flow, breaking down the time and cost for each step in our process:

  1. Cold calling
  2. Getting in touch with the restaurant owner
  3. First meeting
  4. Follow up meeting
  5. Negotiations
  6. Signing agreements
  7. Setting discounts
  8. Adding new deals to the system
  9. Going live with the new deals

It was quite a processWith that out of the way, it was time to crunch some numbers.

Armed with my analysis and research, I proposed a solution that would automate 80% of the current acquisition flow and lower costs by 53%.

Solution part one:

Partnering with another startup to acquire restaurant info

I nurtured a relationship with another startup that had all the information we could ever want about the restaurants we courted (things like owner names, contact details, opening hours, photos, menu, reviews, etc.).

They agreed to share this data with us via an API in exchange for licensing fees. No more cold calling - we could go straight to the owners with our best offers.

Before

Time: 1-3 days. Cost: $7–$8.

After

Time: 0 hrs. Cost: a few cents.

Solution part two:

Create an algorithm to assist sales executives

Initially, a sales executive had to negotiate custom deals with every restaurant. Owners were wary of us at first and there was lots of back-and-forth.

To expedite this process, I created an algorithm that fetches data for similar restaurants using the API our new partners provided.

Owners could see the % discount their direct competitors provided and make an informed decision much more easily. (I mean, who wants to be outdone by their competitors, right?)

Before

Time: 2-4 days. Cost: $20–$22.

After

Time: 1-2 days. Cost: $10–$12.

Solution part three:

Uploading restaurant data with the API.

Finally, I found another use for the API: expediting the onboarding process by pre-populating data in our app. We could upload details with the click of a button and avoid pesky human error.

Before

Time: 1 hour. Cost: $5.

After

Time: a few seconds. Cost: a few cents.

When all was said and done, the results made Ressy hum along much better than before.

Final merchant acquisition cost: ~$15 (a 53% improvement)

Final time to on-board: 3-4 days (a 42% improvement)
